headlineSummary: Anthropic drew the most positive reaction overall, while OpenAI and Google Gemini were in a close tie with mixed-neutral sentiment and limited evidence on Gemini’s side [E1][E2][E4][E5].

ranking.summary: Google Gemini was mixed-neutral but on limited evidence, mainly because one comment criticized its trustworthiness on a signature-related point [E2].
OpenAI came across as mixed-neutral, with a pricing compliment offset by trust and IPO-related criticism [E1][E2][E3].
Anthropic leaned slightly positive, with praise for Opus 4.8 and Opus 4.7’s capability outweighing some trust and business-strategy criticism [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5].

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic and OpenAI - which currently costs me just $100 per provider thanks to their generous subsidized plans for individual subscribers.

annotations.rationale: Calls Anthropic's current subscriber plan 'generous subsidized plans,' which is mild praise for pricing/value.
Calls OpenAI's current subscriber plan 'generous subsidized plans,' which is mild praise for pricing/value.

evidence.excerpt: Ask Claude/ChatGPT/Gemini about the problem, and they will invariably tell you that signatures are less urgent because theyr are not subjective to retroactive compromise.

annotations.rationale: Says Claude 'will invariably tell you' a misleading claim, criticizing reliability on this topic.
Says ChatGPT 'will invariably tell you' a misleading claim, criticizing reliability on this topic.
Says Gemini 'will invariably tell you' a misleading claim, criticizing reliability on this topic.

evidence.excerpt: My bet is that the prices will crash once OpenAI (and/or Antrophic) IPO's have happened. Right now the biggest threat to their IPO's is that people realize that local models are good enough for whatever they're peddling

annotations.rationale: Frames OpenAI around IPO incentives and dismisses its offerings as what they're 'peddling,' a mild negative business-strategy judgment.
The misspelled 'Antrophic' plausibly refers to Anthropic; the comment similarly frames it around IPO motives and 'peddling'.

evidence.excerpt: Opus 4.8 launched and made a bunch of things you mentioned above irrelevant? for example, memory between sessions is way better, dynamic workflows will spin up a ton of agents to do work in parallel

annotations.rationale: Praises Opus 4.8 as making competitors' claims 'irrelevant' and specifically says its memory and workflow behavior are 'way better'.

evidence.excerpt: Opus 4.7 almost one-shot the game (was perfect in 30 mins based on just the wikipedia entry describing the game)

annotations.rationale: Describes Opus 4.7 as 'almost one-shot' and 'perfect in 30 mins,' which is strong praise for capability.
